title: [InstCombine] Only fold extract element to trunc if vector `hasOneUse`

tests.source_program:
declare void @use(i64)

define i8 @index_zero_requires_one_use(i64 %x) {
  %v = bitcast i64 %x to <8 x i8>
  call void @use(i64 %x)
  %r = extractelement <8 x i8> %v, i32 0
  ret i8 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary focuses on variable indices and type generalization, but doesn't mention that index-0 extraction (ShiftAmountC=0) now ALSO requires hasOneUse check. Previously index-0 could optimize without hasOneUse, but the patch restructures the condition to require hasOneUse for ALL cases including ShiftAmountC=0.

alive2.src:
declare void @use(i64)

define i8 @index_zero_requires_one_use(i64 %x) {
  %v = bitcast i64 %x to <8 x i8>
  call void @use(i64 %x)
  %r = extractelement <8 x i8> %v, i32 0
  ret i8 %r
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

declare void @use(i64)

define i8 @index_zero_requires_one_use(i64 %x) {
  call void @use(i64 %x)
  %r = trunc i64 %x to i8
  ret i8 %r
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------

define i8 @index_zero_requires_one_use(i64 %x) {
#0:
  %v = bitcast i64 %x to <8 x i8>
  call void @use(i64 %x)
  %r = extractelement <8 x i8> %v, i32 0
  ret i8 %r
}
=>
declare void @use(i64)

define i8 @index_zero_requires_one_use(i64 %x) {
#0:
  call void @use(i64 %x)
  %r = trunc i64 %x to i8
  ret i8 %r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions floating-point bitcasts but doesn't detail the DestTy->isFloatingPointTy() handling path. This tests float destination conversion after extract optimization.

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define float @float_destination_type(i32 %x) {
  %r = trunc i32 %x to i8
  %f = uitofp i8 %r to float
  ret float %f
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define float @float_destination_type(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %v = bitcast i32 %x to <4 x i8>
  %r = extractelement <4 x i8> %v, i64 0
  %f = uitofp i8 %r to float
  ret float %f
}
=>
define float @float_destination_type(i32 %x) {
#0:
  %r = trunc i32 %x to i8
  %f = uitofp i8 %r to float
  ret float %f
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
declare void @use(i64)

define i8 @zero_index_strict_hasOneUse(i64 %x) {
  %v = bitcast i64 %x to <8 x i8>
  call void @use(i64 %x)
  %r = extractelement <8 x i8> %v, i64 0
  ret i8 %r
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
declare void @use(i64)

define i8 @zero_index_strict_hasOneUse(i64 %x) {
  %v = bitcast i64 %x to <8 x i8>
  call void @use(i64 %x)
  %r = extractelement <8 x i8> %v, i64 0
  ret i8 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't mention the critical hasOneUse requirement change. Before patch, index 0 didn't require hasOneUse. Now it does. This tests the stricter constraint even with zero shift.
